factors.summary: The backlog orders are growing, driven by strong demand for Evidence.com and Axon products, with bookings increasing by 15.4% year-over-year. However, the Net Sales subsection mentions that there were no significant changes in backlog or book-to-bill trends, indicating a possible discrepancy or lack of detailed information.

evidence.text: Order backlog is growing, with bookings increasing by 15.4% year-over-year, driven by strong demand for Evidence.com and Axon products.

Bookings related to Evidence.com and Axon products and services, net of cancellations, were $60.1 million and $52.1 million during the three months ended March 31, 2017 and 2016, respectively, an increase of $8.0 million, or 15.4%. The chart below illustrates the Company's Software and Sensors segment quarterly bookings for each of the previous six fiscal quarters (in thousands):

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with slight decreases in certain product lines offset by new approvals and increases in the Software and Sensors segment driven by technology adoption.

evidence.text: The decrease in sales was primarily attributable to year-end budget appropriations expiring at December 31, 2016 which resulted in higher fourth quarter 2016 revenues as compared to first quarter of 2017. This decrease was partially offset by the Home Office of the UK government approval of the Company's Smart Weapons for sale which resulted in increased revenues in the first quarter of 2017.

The overall increase in the Software and Sensors segment was driven by continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technologies, including the Company's Evidence.com digital evidence management software suite. Revenue related to the Company's on-officer cameras and related accessories increased approximately $4.0 million. The Company is continuing to enter into new contracts with domestic and international agencies seeking to deploy its technologies. Evidence.com revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2017 increased $7.2 million to $11.7 million as compared to the same period in 2016. This increase was primarily driven by an increase in active users on the Company's Evidence.com platform.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards the Software and Sensors segment, which has higher margins. While this shift contributed to gross margin improvements, there have been challenges such as a decrease in sales due to budget expirations and quality control issues affecting product shipments.

evidence.text: Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment were $21.6 million and $23.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017, and December 31, 2016, respectively, a decrease of $2.2 million or 9.1%. The decrease is partially attributable to the expiration of year-end budget appropriations as well as higher backlog of the Axon Flex 2 camera caused by quality control measures resulting in lower shipments during the first quarter than anticipated.

Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment were $21.6 million and $9.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017, and 2016, respectively, an increase of $11.9 million, or 122.5%. The overall increase in the Software and Sensors segment was driven by continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technologies, including the Company's Evidence.com digital evidence management software suite.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments and increased sales suggest a positive trend, despite some challenges.

Net sales were $79.2 million and $55.5 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017 and 2016, respectively, an increase of $23.7 million or 42.7%. International revenues were $14.5 million and $13.1 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017 and 2016, respectively, an increase of $1.4 million or 10.9%. Net sales for the TASER Weapons segment were $57.7 million and $45.8 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017 and 2016, respectively, an increase of $11.8 million or 25.8%. factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed volume trends across its product lines. While certain products, such as the TASER X2 and TASER Pulse and Bolt, are showing significant increases in unit sales, others like the TASER X26P, Axon Flex, Axon Body, and Axon Dock are experiencing decreases. The Software and Sensors segment, particularly Axon products, is driving revenue growth with substantial increases in sales, indicating strong demand in some areas despite declines in others.

evidence.text: Net unit sales for TASER Weapons handles and other products and Axon segment products were as follows: TASER X26P: (26.3%) decrease, TASER X2: 31.8% increase, TASER X26: (19.6%) decrease, TASER Pulse and Bolt: 18.0% increase, Cartridges: 7.5% increase, Axon Flex: (1.5%) decrease, Axon Body: (19.3%) decrease, Axon Dock: (15.2%) decrease, TASER Cam: (56.9%) decrease.

Net unit sales for TASER Weapons handles and other products and Software and Sensors segment products were as follows: [...] Axon Flex 3,101 2,397 704 29.4% [...] Axon Body 20,313 6,198 14,115 227.7% [...] Axon Dock 4,875 1,402 3,473 247.7% [...] The increase in sales was driven by higher demand for on-officer cameras and related accessories.

The overall increase in the Software and Sensors segment was driven by continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technologies, including the Company's Evidence.com digital evidence management software suite. The Company is continuing to enter into new contracts with domestic and international agencies seeking to deploy its technologies. Evidence.com revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2017 increased $7.2 million to $11.7 million as compared to the same period in 2016. This increase was primarily driven by an increase in active users on the Company's Evidence.com platform.

factors.summary: The company's gross margins are under pressure, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment, where costs have risen significantly. While gross margins in absolute terms increased, the margin as a percentage of net sales decreased, indicating a compression. This trend is driven by higher sales volumes and increased costs, leading to a negative trajectory for margins.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $11.8 million to $48.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to $36.9 million for 2016. As a percentage of net sales, gross margin was 61.4% for the three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to 66.5% for 2016.

Within the Software and Sensors segment, cost of products sold and services delivered increased to $12.5 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017 from $4.6 million for the same period in 2016. Cost of products sold and services delivered as a percentage of sales increased to 58.2% for the three months ended March 31, 2017 from 46.9% for the same period in 2016. The overall increase in costs was driven by higher sales volumes, with no significant changes in product costs experienced during the three months ended March 31, 2017 as compared to the same period in 2016.

factors.summary: The company is actively adopting digital technologies, particularly in its Software and Sensors segment, which has led to significant growth in related products and services. However, no specific information on digital automation initiatives was provided in the Net Sales subsection.

evidence.text: The overall increase in the Software and Sensors segment was driven by continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technologies, including the Company's Evidence.com digital evidence management software suite. Revenue related to the Company's on-officer cameras and related accessories increased approximately $4.0 million.

Evidence.com revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2017 increased $7.2 million to $11.7 million as compared to the same period in 2016. This increase was primarily driven by an increase in active users on the Company's Evidence.com platform.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

evidence.text: No specific information on digital technologies or automation initiatives was provided.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in digital technologies and related products suggests successful automation initiatives.

factors.summary: The Company is launching new products, with mixed performance. While the Software and Sensors segment, particularly Axon cameras and Evidence.com services, is performing well with significant revenue growth, there are inherent uncertainties related to new product offerings that are being assessed.

evidence.text: The Company continues to invest in the development of new technologies it will continue to assess the adequacy of its warranty reserve related to inherent uncertainties with new product offerings.

The overall increase in the Software and Sensors segment was driven by continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technologies, including the Company's Evidence.com digital evidence management software suite. Revenue related to the Company's on-officer cameras and related accessories increased approximately $4.0 million. The Company is continuing to enter into new contracts with domestic and international agencies seeking to deploy its technologies. Evidence.com revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2017 increased $7.2 million to $11.7 million as compared to the same period in 2016. This increase was primarily driven by an increase in active users on the Company's Evidence.com platform.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successful new product launches with significant revenue growth, despite some uncertainties.
